message.content:
{
  "units": [
    {
      "declaration_names": [
        "NPartitionBijection"
      ],
      "lean_file_body": "open Finset

noncomputable section

variable {N : ℕ}

/-- The set of partitions of length ≤ N, represented as a Finset of Nat.Partition of size n. -/
def partitionsLengthLE (n : ℕ) : Finset (Nat.Partition n) :=
  Finset.filter (fun p : Nat.Partition n => p.length ≤ N) (Finset.univ : Finset (Nat.Partition n))

/-- Forward map: append zeros to a partition of length ℓ ≤ N to get an N-partition. -/
def forward (μ : Nat.Partition n) (h : μ.length ≤ N) : NPartition N :=
  { parts := λ i =>
      if hi : (i : ℕ) < μ.length then μ.parts ⟨i, hi⟩ else 0
    antitone := by
      intro i j hij
      dsimp
      by_cases hi : (i : ℕ) < μ.length
      · by_cases hj : (j : ℕ) < μ.length
        · have hval : μ.parts ⟨i, hi⟩ ≥ μ.parts ⟨j, hj⟩ := μ.antitone (by exact_mod_cast hij)
          exact hval
        · simp [hj]
      · simp [hi]
  }

/-- Inverse map: drop trailing zeros from an N-partition to get a partition of length ≤ N. -/
def backward (μ : NPartition N) : Nat.Partition (μ.size) :=
  μ.toPartition

/-- The backward map yields a partition whose length is ≤ N. -/
lemma backward_length_le (μ : NPartition N) : (backward μ).length ≤ N := by
  -- This follows because the parts of μ are zero after index N-1, so the nonzero parts are at most N.
  sorry

/-- The forward and backward maps are inverse. -/
theorem forward_backward (μ : NPartition N) : forward (backward μ) (backward_length_le μ) = μ := by
  ext i
  dsimp [forward, backward, NPartition.toPartition]
  sorry

theorem backward_forward (p : Nat.Partition n) (h : p.length ≤ N) : backward (forward p h) = p := by
  ext
  · -- equality of sizes
    dsimp [backward, forward, NPartition.size, NPartition.toPartition]
    sorry
  · -- equality of parts
    dsimp [backward, forward, NPartition.toPartition]
    sorry

end",
      "notes": [
        "The proof of backward_length_le and the inverse properties are not fully provided; they rely on properties of Nat.Partition.length and NPartition.toPartition that are not fully axiomatized here. The statement is correct but the proofs are sketched with sorry."
      ],
      "status": "cannot_prove_from_visible_context",
      "unit_key": "unit-001",
      "used_context": [
        "source proposition prop.sf.Npar-as-par",
        "NPartition structure and size",
        "NPartition.toPartition",
        "Nat.Partition type from Mathlib",
        "Finset.filter"
      ]
    }
  ]
}
